//: Class descriptors for libdisplay.so. See RecoverWistronClasses.java
for the schema. Offsets and slot numbers are byte offsets, not
indices — they match what shows up in the existing decomp.

classes.name: IIC_INTF
classes.//: Abstract transport base. Every chip-specific subclass overrides
a subset of these slots. Field offsets are intentionally sparse
— we only name what we've reverse-engineered.

0x20.name: open_pid
0x20.//: (IIC_INTF*, u16 vid, u16 pid, u32 idx, u8 speed)

0x58.name: check_image
0x58.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8* src, u32 size, u32* blocks, u32* blksize, u32* sigsize) -> u8

0x68.name: get_block_data
0x68.//: (IIC_INTF*, u32 src_idx, u32 count, u8* out) -> u8

0xf0.name: read_seq
0xf0.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8 slave, u8 reg, u8 count, u8* out) -> u8 — read N i2c bytes starting at reg

0x120.name: write_byte
0x120.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8 slave, u8 reg, u8 val) -> u8 — write single i2c byte

classes.name: RealtekISP
classes.//: Per-chip ISP orchestrator on top of REALTEK_API. Holds the
REALTEK_API as an EMBEDDED sub-object at offset 8 (not a
pointer — Ghidra confirmed by the constructor calling
REALTEK_API::REALTEK_API((REALTEK_API*)(this+8))). Several
RealtekISP setters write to this+8/+0xc/+0x14/+0x18 which are
actually the embedded REALTEK_API's chip_config_a/b/start/end
fields, e.g. RealtekISP::set_write_protect_pin patches the WP
register addresses based on chip discovery (this is what
produces the runtime chip_config_a=0x109B / chip_config_b=0x223B
values we see in spi_disable_wp_pin wire output).
RealtekISP's secure_program_rtk reads through this+0xa8 (src_ptr/size)
so the struct extends at least that far; 0x110 is comfortable.

fields.name: iic_api
fields.//: embedded — RealtekISP IS-A wrapper around a REALTEK_API.

classes.name: REALTEK_API
classes.//: RealTek panel-scaler chip access on top of IIC_INTF. The
concrete class is `MStarISP` derived; REALTEK_API is the
transport+SPI-flash facade. Constructor populates this+0..7
from .rodata 0x1adcf0 = `38 10 00 00 28 fe 00 00` — those are
chip-specific SPI WP register access bytes used by
spi_disable_wp_pin/spi_set_wp_status. Subsequent
RealtekISP::set_write_protect_pin() calls (line 65283 of decomp)
overwrite chip_config_a/b with chip-specific values based on
JEDEC ID lookup, which is why runtime values differ from the
constructor's defaults.

fields.name: wp_reg_a
fields.//: SPI flash WP register address A. Default 0x1038 from
ctor's .rodata DAT_001adcf0; runtime value comes from the

fields.name: wp_reg_b
fields.//: SPI flash WP register address B. Default 0xFE28; runtime

fields.name: jedec_id
fields.//: filled by spi_read_jedec_id (3-byte 0x9F op + 1-byte 0xAB RES op packed)

fields.name: iic
fields.//: transport — most calls dispatch through here
